MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-14-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Union Carbide Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-6 and 8 (exposure/product identification, bulk supplier defense, sophisticated user defense, and punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 7 (false representation). The declaration of plaintiff and deposition testimony of Mr. Hamilton create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos attributable to defendant. The D'Amico objections to plaintiff's declaration are overruled. The purportedly inconsistent deposition testimony cited by defendant only concerns plaintiff's use of the red dot product, not the use of the red dot product by others. The bulk supplier defense is not available to a supplier of raw asbestos in asbestos exposure cases because asbestos is inherently dangerous and raw asbestos fibers do not change when they become a component of an asbestos-containing product. (Garza; Jenkins; Arena). The sophisticated user defense does not shield defendant from liability. (Stewart). Defendant failed to present evidence showing what knowledge Hamilton Materials had about the hazards of asbestos and what warnings, if any, it provided to Hamilton Materials. Plaintiff submitted more than ample evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant made misrepresentations about and/or withheld material information concerning its knowledge of the hazards of asbestos and thus whether it acted in conscious disregard for the safety of persons who were exposed to its asbestos. Plaintiff represents that he will dismiss his cause of action for false representation. Plaintiff is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-14-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant DAP, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 (exposure/causation) and granted as to issues 2 and 3 (false representation and punitive damages). The declarations of plaintiff and Dr. Bruch and defendant's responses to GO 129 interrogatories create triable issues whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos from painter's caulk and architectural caulk attributable to defendant and whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his asbestos-related disease. Defendant's objections to the declarations of plaintiff and Dr. Bruch are overruled. Plaintiff appears to concede that his claim for false representation and request for punitive damages lack merit. Plaintiff is to prepare an order. .Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
